sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: mb-cell-collapse-toggle visually missing and non-functional after disk-load restore on multi-table pages — two compounding bugs in the rebuild path of _artBuildMultiRowArtCell (taken when a ul.mb-caa-art-ul already exists in the deserialized cell HTML): (a) Invisible toggle: saveTableDataToDisk serializes only cell innerHTML and colSpan/rowSpan, NOT the <td> className attribute. The mb-has-collapse-toggle class that gives the <td> position:relative (required as a CSS positioning context for the toggle's position:absolute) is therefore never present on deserialized cells. Without it the toggle is absolutely positioned relative to the nearest positioned ancestor (the table or body) rather than its own cell, so it floats outside the visible cell area and is effectively invisible. The first-build path always calls artCell.classList.add('mb-has-collapse-toggle') but the rebuild path did not — fixed by adding the same call to the rebuild path. (b) Non-functional toggle: ensureCollapseDelegate(table) was only called in the first-build path. For pages without explicit collapsableColumns configured (such as artist-releasegroups), initCollapsableColumns returns before calling ensureCollapseDelegate, so the tbody click-delegation listener is never installed and clicking the toggle has no effect. Fixed by adding ensureCollapseDelegate(artCell.closest('table')) to the rebuild path as well. Additionally: if the toggle span was absent from the serialized HTML entirely (disk files from before the multi-row feature, or stripped by an older pipeline), the rebuild path previously did nothing when toggle was null; it now creates the span fresh with the correct className and role attributes before setting the glyph and aria state.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Two bugs remaining after 9.99.156 on disk-load restore: (1) mb-cell-collapse-toggle still not rendered inside multi-row CAA/EAA art cells after loading from disk (new or same session) — root cause: getCleanCellHtml only strips transient markers at save time, so disk files saved before 9.99.155 still carry data-caa-enriched='1' on art anchors; at load time _artEnrichIcon sees the marker and returns immediately, _artBuildMultiRowArtCell is never called, and the toggle serialized in the old HTML (in whatever glyph state it was saved) is never re-wired with a live ensureCollapseDelegate. (2) CAA/EAA global toggle button still missing from the h2 header in new-session disk-load — root cause: _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton anchors the button with countStat.after(btn) where countStat is h2 .mb-row-count-stat; during loadTableDataFromDisk this element does not exist when initCaaPics runs inside the first await renderGroupedTable(groupedRows) call (the count stat is only created later by updateH2Count), and it still does not exist when initCaaPics runs inside the runFilter() call that immediately follows (updateH2Count in runFilter creates the stat after renderGroupedTable returns, but the re-anchor loop added in 9.99.156 finds no buttons to re-anchor because the button was never created); the subsequent updateH2Count(loadedRowCount) call at the bottom of loadTableDataFromDisk recreates the stat once more, still with no button. Fixed both bugs by: (a) extracting all cleanup logic from getCleanCellHtml into a new private helper _stripTransientCellState(el) that operates in-place; getCleanCellHtml continues to use it on a cloneNode(true) copy; (b) calling _stripTransientCellState(td) on every freshly-deserialized <td> in both the multi-table and single-table branches of loadTableDataFromDisk, so old disk files are cleaned at load time regardless of when they were saved; (c) adding an explicit _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(CAA_CTX) and _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(EAA_CTX) call immediately after the final updateH2Count(loadedRowCount) in loadTableDataFromDisk, at which point the h2 .mb-row-count-stat is guaranteed to exist and will not be recreated again; in a same-session load the buttons already exist so this is a cheap idempotent update call.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Four bugs remaining after 9.99.155 on multi-table disk-load restore: (1) New-session disk-load: CAA/EAA global toggle button missing from h2 header entirely — root cause: _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton runs at the tail of the first renderGroupedTable call in loadTableDataFromDisk, before updateH2Count has created the .mb-row-count-stat span, so it exits early ('not found — deferring'); on the second renderGroupedTable call (inside runFilter) the count stat exists and the button is created with countStat.after(btn), but runFilter's own updateH2Count call immediately after renderGroupedTable removes and recreates the count stat span, leaving the button floating before the new span. Fixed by adding a re-anchor pass at the end of updateH2Count's insertion block that collects all direct-child .mb-global-art-toggle-btn and [id$="-global-retry"] elements of the target h2 in DOM order and re-inserts them in reverse order immediately after the new count stat span, ensuring correct position on every updateH2Count invocation regardless of call order. (2) Same-session disk-load: CAA/EAA global toggle button rendered before the .mb-row-count-stat span in the h2 — same root cause and same fix as (1): updateH2Count's count-stat replacement displaced the already-existing button. (3) Double cache-hint emoji (e.g. two 🔵 or ⚠️ spans) in CAA art cells after disk-load — root cause: the 9.99.155 getCleanCellHtml fix incorrectly 'unwrapped' .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap by moving its inner .mb-art-cache-hint-col child out to the parent element and then removing the wrapper; on disk-load _artLoadIcon saw the orphaned inner span as the anchor's nextElementSibling, found it was not an .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap, created a brand-new wrapper, inserted it after the anchor, and added a second hint span inside it — producing two hint spans side by side. Fixed by removing the entire .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap element (and all its descendants) rather than unwrapping it; an additional pass removes any pre-existing standalone .mb-art-cache-hint-col spans to clean up disk files saved by the incorrect 9.99.155 unwrap logic. (4) mb-cell-collapse-toggle ▶/◀ button non-functional after disk-load when a multi-row art cell was saved in expanded state: two compounding bugs — (a) getCleanCellHtml used the wrong Unicode code point \u25C4 (◄ BLACK LEFT-POINTING POINTER) to detect the expanded-state glyph instead of \u25C0 (◀ BLACK LEFT-POINTING TRIANGLE), so the toggle was never reset to ▶ during save; (b) the _artBuildMultiRowArtCell rebuild branch only updated toggle.title and aria-label without resetting toggle.textContent or aria-expanded, so a ◀ loaded from disk remained ◀ after rebuild — clicking it attempted to collapse an already-collapsed list and appeared to do nothing. Fixed by correcting the Unicode check to \u25C0 and by adding toggle.textContent = '\u25B6' and toggle.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false') to the rebuild branch so the toggle is always reset to collapsed state when _artBuildMultiRowArtCell rebuilds the image-li list from fresh API data.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items:
Five bugs on disk-load restore (both same-session and new-session) when sa_art_idb_enable=false: (1) mb-cell-collapse-toggle span missing after disk load and not restored by the ⟳ retry button — root cause: data-caa-enriched on art anchors survived serialization, causing _artEnrichIcon to return immediately so _artBuildMultiRowArtCell was never called and the ul/li structure deserialized from HTML had no live toggle or event listeners. (2) Expanded artwork icons in multi-row CAA cells visible after disk load but not hoverable — same root cause as (1): because _artEnrichIcon was short-circuited, _artBuildImageLi was never called on the deserialized rows, so no mouseenter/mouseleave listeners were attached to the per-image elements. (3) Previously expanded multi-row art cells remained expanded after disk load instead of restoring to collapsed state — li.mb-caa-art-li-image items saved with display:'' (expanded) were not reset to display:none on serialization. (4 & 5) ERG (▶ Expand Release-Groups) ghost sub-table after disk load: when an ERG button was clicked before saving, the dynamically-fetched inline (appended as a direct child of the
by the button's closure) was serialized verbatim into the cell HTML; on reload initExpandRGsFeature injected a new live button whose closure referenced a fresh empty table, but the ghost table from the saved HTML remained orphaned beside it — clicking the new button appended a second empty table rather than toggling the ghost. Fixed in all five cases by extending getCleanCellHtml() to strip the following transient state from the cloned cell before serialization: (a) data-caa-enriched / data-eaa-enriched from art anchors; (b) data-caa-multi-built / data-eaa-multi-built from art elements; (c) data-caa-inline-done / data-eaa-inline-done from elements; (d) li.mb-caa-art-li-image / li.mb-eaa-art-li-image items reset to display:none; (e) .mb-cell-collapse-toggle reset from ◀ to ▶ with matching title/aria attributes; (f) background-image / background-size removed from caa-icon / eaa-icon / artwork-icon spans; (g) .mb-caa-inline-ph / .mb-eaa-inline-ph placeholder spans removed entirely; (h) .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap wrappers unwrapped and .mb-art-cache-hint-big / .mb-art-cache-hint-inline overlays removed; (i) [data-erg-btn] innerHTML reset from ▼ to ▶; (j) direct-child elements of the cloned
removed (ERG ghost sub-tables); (k) data-erg-injected stripped so initExpandRGsFeature re-injects live buttons on the deserialized rows.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA big-picture strip (bigbox) on artist-releasegroups pages showed only a small fraction of the available cover art images when sa_art_idb_enable=false, and the toggle button badge reflected the same wrong count (e.g. 12 instead of 39 for a 45-row 'Album + Compilation' sub-table). Root cause: _artInitBigPics fired img.src (non-IDB path) or _artFetchCachedImage (IDB path) for every bigbox image across all sub-tables in a single synchronous forEach pass. On a large artist page with 31 sub-tables this produced a burst of hundreds of simultaneous HTTP requests to coverartarchive.org; the browser silently dropped or rate-limited the excess. Because the img.onerror handler removes the wrapper from the bigbox without emitting any log message, the missing images were invisible and the badge counted only the successfully-loaded subset. The small-pic icon column and the inline Title-column thumbnails were unaffected because both already route their img.src assignments through _caaQueue (concurrency=4). Fixed by wrapping both the non-IDB (native img.onload) and the IDB (_artFetchCachedImage) bigbox fetch paths in the same _caaQueue throttle, exactly mirroring the pattern used by _artInitSmallPics and _artInitInlinePics. The retry button (⟳) already rebuilt the bigbox from scratch with cache-busted URLs which happen to fire after the initial burst subsides, explaining why it appeared to 'fix' the strip. After this fix the strip populates correctly on the first render without needing a retry.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Inline Title-column thumbnail hover preview non-functional after new-session disk-load restore: the hover-wiring block in Case C of _artInitInlinePics set existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr]='1' on the first render's ph span before enqueueing the C2 refetch. cloneNode(true) — used by the second renderGroupedTable pass inside runFilter() to rebuild tds from groupedRows — copied all dataset attributes including hoverWiredAttr to the cloned ph spans. The second render's Case C then evaluated !existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr] as false and skipped the addEventListener calls entirely, leaving the live second-render ph element with no mouseenter/mouseleave listeners. Fixed by replacing the guarded if block with an unconditional delete existingPh.dataset[hoverWiredAttr] followed by the listener attachment, so any cloneNode-inherited value is always cleared before re-wiring. The hoverWiredAttr idempotency guard is unnecessary inside Case C in any case: Case C only fires when td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr] is absent, and every exit path of Case C stamps that marker (C1 immediately, C2 after successful refetch), making it impossible for Case C to re-enter on the same physical DOM node — and therefore impossible for duplicate listeners to accumulate.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Inline Title-column thumbnails invisible after new-session disk-load restore — cache-hint emoji rendered but remained display:none: the C2 path introduced in 9.99.151 set td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr]='1' eagerly at the top of Case C before enqueueing the refetch. The disk-load pipeline calls renderGroupedTable twice in immediate succession — first to build the initial DOM, then again synchronously via runFilter() which clones every td with cloneNode(true). cloneNode copies all dataset attributes, so every cloned td in the second render already carried the 'done' marker. The second render's initCaaInlinePics() found marker present + ph span present → Case A → skip, while initCaaPics() simultaneously replaced _caaQueue with a fresh instance, abandoning all first-render refetch tasks still pending in the old queue. Net result: queue tasks from the first render ran on detached nodes with no visible effect; queue tasks for the second render were never enqueued; all images remained display:none and only the serialised cache-hint emoji from the saved HTML was visible. Fixed by removing the eager marker assignment from C2: td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr]='1' is now set inside the _artFetchCachedImage .then() callback, after existingImg.src and existingImg.style.display='inline' are written, and only when existingImg.isConnected is true (guarding against tasks that complete after a subsequent tbody.innerHTML='' detaches the node). The second render's cloned tds therefore carry no marker, fall into Case C again, and trigger their own refetch on the live DOM nodes that are actually rendered. C1 (same-session restore, blobIsAlive=true) still stamps the marker immediately because the image is already visible and no async path is involved. The native img.onload fallback path (IDB disabled) received the same deferred-marker and isConnected treatment, and its event listeners were converted to { once: true } to prevent accumulation.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Inline Title-column thumbnails rendered as browser broken-image icons on the first load in a new browser session after loading data from disk: the disk-save pipeline serialises cell HTML verbatim, including the elements written by _artInitInlinePics. blob: URLs are session-scoped — they are revoked on pagehide and unknown to the new session's _artIdbBlobUrls Set. Case C of the _artInitInlinePics idempotency logic detects 'marker absent, placeholder present' and, before this fix, simply re-stamped the td-level marker and returned immediately, leaving the broken blob: URL in img.src untouched. Fixed by splitting Case C into two sub-cases keyed on a single _artIdbBlobUrls.has(img.src) check: C1 (same-session restore, blob: URL still live) preserves the previous behaviour — image is already rendering, hover listeners are re-wired, function returns; C2 (new-session restore, blob: URL absent from _artIdbBlobUrls) immediately clears img.src to '' and hides the to remove the broken-image icon, then enqueues a refetchTask through _caaQueue (respecting the shared concurrency budget) which calls _artFetchCachedImage(imgurl) and writes the resulting objectUrl back into the existing element inside the existing ph span. Reusing the existing DOM nodes is essential: falling through to the fresh-injection code path below Case C would append a second alongside the first one, producing duplicate thumbnails per cell. Both C1 and C2 attach hover listeners unconditionally (guarded by the per-placeholder ctx.key+'HoverWired' idempotency attribute) so the hover-preview fix from 9.99.150 is preserved for both sub-cases. A native img.onload/onerror fallback path inside refetchTask handles the IDB-disabled case identically to the original fresh-injection loadTask.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Hover preview popup blank / never populated when sa_art_idb_enable=true (affects both CAA icon column and inline Title-column thumbnails): _showArtHoverPreview assigned the plain coverartarchive.org / eventartarchive.org network URL directly to previewImg.src. With IDB enabled, thumbnails are fetched via GM_xmlhttpRequest in Tampermonkey's extension background context; the browser's page-level HTTP cache is never populated for those URLs. When hover fired, previewImg.src triggered a fresh page-context request that had to follow the non-cacheable 302 redirect to archive.org — a 2–4 s round-trip — so the popup rendered as an empty white box and the image arrived only after the user had already moved away. Fixed by routing through _artFetchCachedImage(imgUrl) when sa_art_idb_enable is true: the big-picture strip has already loaded the same bigSize URL into the session Map (_artIdbMemCache) via the _artInitBigPics IDB path, so _artFetchCachedImage resolves synchronously in the same microtask and previewImg.src receives a live blob: URL with no network round-trip. The div.style.display !== 'none' guard inside the .then() ensures a late-resolving fetch (cold IDB miss) does not update a popup the user has already left. The original img.src assignment path is retained for the IDB-disabled case. Simultaneously fixed: inline Title-column thumbnails on same-session disk-load restore had no hover listeners attached — the mouseenter/mouseleave wiring lives inside the loadTask() closure, which Case C of the _artInitInlinePics idempotency logic never called (it returned immediately after re-stamping the marker). Fixed by iterating all existing ph spans in Case C, re-deriving bigUrl from the entity link href, and attaching listeners on each ph element, guarded by a per-placeholder ctx.key+'HoverWired' dataset attribute to prevent accumulation on repeated _artInitInlinePics calls.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA column permanently empty on artist-relationships and similar pages where MusicBrainz does not embed span.caa-icon in table rows: the script has two independent artwork-detection pipelines — the bigbox/inline pipeline scans plain entity links (a[href*='/release-group/']) and works correctly, but the CAA-column/small-icon/count-badge pipeline is driven by iconSel which requires 'td a[href$="/cover-art"] > span.caa-icon'. On artist-relationships rows the source Title cell contains only a bare with no span.caa-icon child, so iconSel matches nothing, _artInitSmallPics and _artEnrichTable both enqueue 0 items, and the entire CAA column stays blank. Fixed by adding Path C to the caa ColumnDataExtractor: when neither Path A (artwork anchor) nor Path B (bare icon span) finds anything, but a release-group or release link exists in the source cell, a synthetic is created in the CAA cell — the same DOM structure Path A produces — enabling both pipelines to work identically. The sort key is conservatively written as 'no' at extraction time (artwork unknown); _artEnrichIcon flips it to 'yes' after the CAA JSON API confirms count > 0, covering both the network-fetch path and the IDB early-return path. sortKeyClass property added to CAA_CTX and EAA_CTX to allow _artEnrichIcon to locate the hidden sort-key span without a hardcoded class name.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA toggle button thumbnail permanently stuck as browser broken-image when sa_art_idb_enable=false: the HTML spec sets HTMLImageElement.complete=true as soon as the browser finishes a fetch attempt — regardless of success or failure (404, network error). The toggle button thumb is created with thumb.src=firstImgUrl (the first entity link found in the table). If that URL returns 404 (release-group has no CAA artwork), complete becomes true and naturalWidth stays 0 (broken-image state). When a bigbox image for a different entity in the same table later fires its img.onload, the handler's guard '!thumb.complete' evaluates to false (complete=true for the broken thumb), so thumb.src is never replaced and the broken image persists even after the badge correctly increments. Fixed by replacing !thumb.complete with (!thumb.complete || thumb.naturalWidth===0) at all three guard sites: (1) per-table toggle button img.onload in _artInitBigPics, (2) global toggle button img.onload in the same callback, (3) _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton re-render branch (covers the 'Reload all CAA images' retry path, where the existing broken thumb was never reset before _artInitBigPics ran again).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Navigation guard (initNavigationGuard Guard 1) erroneously showed 'You are about to leave this page' alert when clicking the 'Save to Disk' button: triggerStandardDownload() creates a temporary <a href='blob:…' download='…'> element and calls .click() on it programmatically to trigger the browser file-save dialog. Guard 1's capture-phase click listener intercepted that synthetic click, found an anchor with a non-same-page href, resolved the blob: URL as an apparent cross-origin navigation, and called confirmNavigation() — showing the alert even though no page navigation occurs. Fixed by adding an early-return guard in Guard 1 immediately after the javascript:/fragment/empty-href checks: if anchor.hasAttribute('download') OR href.startsWith('blob:') the listener returns without calling confirmNavigation(). Both conditions are true for every download anchor created by triggerStandardDownload() and the three export helpers; checking either is sufficient, but both are checked as a belt-and-braces guard so the save/export flow is never interrupted if one condition changes in the future.

Added @connect archive.org and @connect *.archive.org to the userscript header: coverartarchive.org and eventartarchive.org redirect image requests via HTTP 302 to archive.org, which in turn redirects to an arbitrary *.archive.org CDN cache server (e.g. ia803408.us.archive.org). GM_xmlhttpRequest follows these redirects automatically, but Tampermonkey blocks any redirect whose target host is not listed in @connect. Without these two entries every IDB-cache-miss image fetch would fail at the archive.org redirect step, making the IDB cache effectively non-functional for cold (first-time) fetches.

sections.label: ✨ New Features
items: IndexedDB persistent artwork cache (CAA / EAA): all three artwork image-loading pipelines (_artLoadIcon for icon-column thumbnails, _artInitBigPics for the big-picture strip, _artInitInlinePics for inline Release/Title column thumbnails) and the JSON-metadata enrichment pipeline (_artEnrichIcon for image-count badges and multi-row art cells) now consult a three-tier cache before touching the network. Tier 1 is the existing per-session in-memory Map (_artIdbMemCache) that covers sort/filter re-renders within the same page load at zero cost. Tier 2 is a new IndexedDB database ('vz-mb-saed-art-cache', two object stores: 'images' for raw Blobs keyed by normalised https:// URL, 'metadata' for archive JSON keyed by entity path) that survives page reloads and Load-from-Disk restores — the path that benefits most since almost no network round-trips are needed. Tier 3 is the network: images are fetched via GM_xmlhttpRequest (CORS-bypass, two new @connect directives added: coverartarchive.org and eventartarchive.org) and stored as Blobs in IDB; metadata is fetched via the existing fetch() call and also stored in IDB, including the 404/zero-count sentinel so even absent-artwork entities skip the network on subsequent loads. Blob: object URLs are tracked in _artIdbBlobUrls and revoked en-masse on the pagehide event to release Blob memory. All IDB operations (open, get, put, delete, cursor sweep) are wrapped in standard IDB request/transaction helpers (_artOpenIdb, _artIdbGet, _artIdbPut, _artIdbDelete) with full error handling; failures fall through silently to the next tier. Expired records are evicted lazily on read (TTL check in _artFetchCachedImage and _artIdbGetMetadata) and proactively via a background cursor sweep (_artIdbSweepExpired) scheduled with requestIdleCallback (5-second setTimeout fallback) after each initCaaPics() call so stale data is never visible but the sweep never competes with the image load queue. IDB hits are reported to the Resource Timing cache-hint indicator system as a new 'idb' status (emoji 🗄️, label 'IndexedDB cache hit') in all three display locations (icon column, bigbox strip, inline thumbnail); since blob: URLs produce no PerformanceResourceEntry the RT API is intentionally skipped for IDB hits and the status is synthesised directly. Three new settings control the feature: sa_art_idb_enable (master toggle, default true), sa_art_idb_image_ttl_days (Blob TTL in days, default 30, range 1–365), sa_art_idb_metadata_ttl_days (JSON TTL in days, default 7, range 1–90), placed in a new '🗄️ ART ARCHIVE INDEXEDDB CACHE' settings section between the existing CAA/EAA and Resource Timing sections. When sa_art_idb_enable is false or cacheBust=true (force-retry path) all three load sites fall back to their original native img.src / fetch() paths with no behaviour change. The IDB database can be inspected and cleared via browser DevTools → Application → Storage → IndexedDB → 'vz-mb-saed-art-cache'.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA / EAA column unique-values dropdown — 'Cell structure' artwork-presence entries missing when multi-row art cells exist: when a CAA or EAA column is also listed in collapsableColumns and at least one cell contains multiple artwork thumbnails (ul.mb-caa-art-ul / ul>li structure), totalMultiRow becomes >0 and the isCollapsableCol branch in the synthetic-entries block fires, causing the else-if (isCaaOrEaaCol) branch to be skipped entirely — the '✓ has artwork' / '✗ no artwork' quick-filter entries were never rendered. Fixed by appending the artwork-presence makeArtItem() entries inside the isCollapsableCol branch immediately after the collapse-state entries, guarded by isCaaOrEaaCol && (caaYesCount > 0 || caaNoCount > 0). The else-if (isCaaOrEaaCol) branch is retained unchanged for the common case where the column has no multi-row structure (all cells are single-artwork or art-absent, so all three structural counters are zero and the isCollapsableCol guard is false). The block comment above the synthetic-entries section has been updated to document both the combined and the single-path cases.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Keyboard focus ring missing on Tab-reachable elements: all interactive elements injected by the script (h1 action-bar buttons, H2 filter-bar utility buttons, H3 sub-table buttons including CAA/EAA toggles, retry buttons, resize and column-visibility buttons, per-column filter inputs, the global and pre-filter inputs, the quickfilter input inside the unique-values dropdown, sort-icon buttons, column-collapse header buttons, and H2/H3 section headers) now display a visible two-layer focus indicator when reached via Tab or Shift+Tab. The ring is rendered as a 2px solid #1a73e8 outline (with 2px offset) plus a matching soft halo (box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(26,115,232,0.28)) so it follows the element's border-radius. !important is used on both properties to override MusicBrainz's global 'outline: 0' reset for <button> elements and any inline cssText that would otherwise win on specificity. :focus-visible is used as the primary selector so mouse clicks do not produce a ring in modern browsers. A @supports not selector(:focus-visible) block provides the identical rules under plain :focus for older browsers that lack :focus-visible support.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: CAA / EAA column unique-values dropdown: a 'Cell structure' section with '✓ has artwork' and '✗ no artwork' quick-filter entries is now shown at the top of the dropdown for CAA and EAA columns, in addition to the regular alphabetically-sorted value list. The entries are counted from the visible rows' pre-baked mb-caa-sort-key 'yes' / 'no' sort values, so their occurrence badges always reflect the current filter state. Clicking '✓ has artwork' sets the column filter to the text 'yes' (same as clicking that value in the regular list); clicking '✗ no artwork' sets it to 'no'. The section uses the existing makeArtItem() helper which calls applyUniqVal() directly — no new filter mode was required. The section is separated from the regular value list by the standard synBox divider rule and only rendered when at least one of the two counts is non-zero. Column detection uses th.dataset.colName (stamped by makeTableSortableUnified) with a stripped-textContent fallback, and fires for both 'CAA' and 'EAA' column names.

Tab key focus-trap (initNavigationGuard Guard 2) reworked to intercept every Tab and Shift+Tab keystroke unconditionally, preventing focus from ever escaping to the browser chrome (address bar, toolbars, tabs). Previously e.preventDefault() was only called at the boundary elements (first / last), meaning a single Tab press from an element not yet in the focusable list could still hand focus to the browser. The new implementation calls e.preventDefault() first on every Tab keydown, then computes the next index via focusable.indexOf(active) ± 1 with wrap-around. Forward Tab from the last element wraps to index 0; Shift+Tab from the first element (or any element not in the list) wraps to the last. The guard only runs when isLoaded is true, so it is completely inert before the consolidated table is rendered.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: Navigation guard (initNavigationGuard) completely reworked. Three independent guards are now installed under a single idempotent _mbNavGuardInstalled flag: (1) Anchor click guard — page-wide, identical to the expansion introduced in 9.99.141. (2) Tab key focus-trap — a new keydown capture listener intercepts Tab and Shift+Tab: when the focused element is the last (or first for Shift+Tab) visible, enabled focusable element on the page, the default browser behaviour of moving focus into the browser chrome (address bar, toolbar) is suppressed and focus wraps around to the first (or last) page element instead. Focusable elements are collected via a combined selector (a[href], button:not([disabled]), input, select, textarea, [tabindex]) and filtered for visibility; elements with tabIndex > 0 are sorted to the front per the HTML spec. (3) Merge-form submit-button guard — a second click capture listener detects button clicks inside any <form> that contains a div.list-merge-buttons-row-container (the MusicBrainz 'Add all releases for merging' / 'Merge release groups' action forms). Clicking one of these buttons submits the form and navigates away; the guard intercepts the click and shows the standard confirmation dialog. Buttons whose formtarget='_blank' open in a new tab and are not guarded. The human-readable form label is extracted from the embedded <script type='application/json'> for the debug log. JSDoc updated to describe all three guards.

initNavigationGuard debug log updated: the final installed message now reads 'Navigation guard installed (anchor + Tab-trap + merge-form).' and each guard emits a dedicated debug line on trigger/cancel.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: H2 / H3 hover background color split into two independent settings: sa_ui_h2_h3_hover_bg has been replaced by sa_ui_h2_hover_bg (applies to .mb-toggle-h2:hover, default #f9f9f9) and sa_ui_h3_hover_bg (applies to .mb-toggle-h3:hover, default #f9f9f9). Both are color_picker type. The previously combined CSS rule '.mb-toggle-h3:hover, .mb-toggle-h2:hover { … }' has been split into two separate per-class rules so each header level can be styled independently. Descriptions updated to reference only their respective CSS class.

New 'wiencek' columnEraser sentinel added to applyColumnErasers() — Strategy 3: when the eraser string 'wiencek' is present in an erasers array, all <div class="suggested-work"> and <div class="work"> containers are removed from the target cell. These divs are injected by Michael Wiencek's 'MusicBrainz: Expand/collapse release groups' userscript on Artist-Recordings pages and carry 'Looking for matching work…' / 'Suggested work: …' hints and 'live recording of …' relationship lines that clutter the consolidated Name column. The 'wiencek' sentinel is now excluded from the textErasers array (alongside 'jesus2099') so it does not accidentally trigger the span-text erasure path.

artist-recordings page definition updated: { sourceColumn: 'Name', erasers: ['wiencek'] } added to features.columnErasers. This removes the suggested-work / work divs from every Name cell in the consolidated Artist-Recordings table.

Navigation guard (initNavigationGuard) now intercepts anchor clicks everywhere on the rendered page, not just inside table.tbl elements. Previously clicking a link in the MusicBrainz header, sidebar, breadcrumbs, or any other element outside the consolidated table would navigate away without confirmation, silently discarding all loaded data, filters, sorts and column settings. The table.tbl containment check has been removed so the guard fires for any link on the page that resolves to a different URL. JSDoc and inline comments updated accordingly.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: All color configuration parameters in configSchema that used type: 'color' have been changed to type: 'color_picker'. Affected settings: sa_ui_h2_bg, sa_ui_h3_bg, sa_ui_thead_th_bg, sa_ui_thead_th_color, sa_ui_thead_filter_row_bg. (sa_ui_h2_hover_bg and sa_ui_h3_hover_bg were already color_picker from their introduction in 9.99.141.)

Configurable synthetic column header background color (sa_ui_thead_th_synthetic_bg, default #b8c8b8): a new color_picker setting in the UI APPEARANCE section controls the background color of all injected synthetic table column headers (Event-Type, Event-Date, Country, Date, MB-Name, Comment, CAA, EAA, etc.). The default is a darker greenish grey, visually distinct from the standard header background (#e8e8e8), making synthetic columns immediately identifiable at a glance. The previously hard-coded '#d3d3d3' value in cleanupHeaders() has been replaced by Lib.settings.sa_ui_thead_th_synthetic_bg.

Four new filename construction options in the LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK configSchema section: sa_filename_prefix (string, default 'MB-') sets the prefix of every generated save filename; sa_filename_include_detail (boolean, default true) controls whether the optional detail segment (multi-button label or filtered-relationship slug) is included; sa_filename_include_row_count (boolean, default true) controls whether the row count is included in parentheses; sa_filename_include_timestamp (boolean, default true) controls whether the ISO-8601 timestamp is appended.

Save filename format redesigned (v9.99.140+). The new format is: -()-
[-(detailSlug)][(-rowCount)][_timestamp].json.gz. Changes: (1) The entity name is extracted from the h1 element (plus any trailing text after the , e.g. an event date), and inserted in parentheses between the entity-type prefix and the page-section suffix. (2) Every dash-separated word in the pageType slug is Title-cased (e.g. 'artist-relationships' → 'Artist-Relationships'). (3) The row count is wrapped in parentheses rather than appearing as a bare number. (4) The timestamp is separated from the rest by an underscore rather than a dash. Example: MB-Artist-(Bruce Springsteen)-Relationships-(1329)_2026-03-13T11-54-51.json.gz. The load-path detailSlugFromFilename() fallback (strategy 2) has been updated to recognise the new format and bail out early (entity-name files start with '(' after the prefix and are handled by strategy 1 via data.buttonLabel), preserving backward compatibility with files saved in the old format.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: applyH1CommentSpanRelocation: the final-page pass was silently deleting the alias line injected by the initial-page pass. Root cause: the stale #mb-h1-alias-line removal was executed unconditionally at the top of the function, before the source-data guards; when the final pass ran it removed the existing alias line, then found no <span class='comment'> in the h1 (consumed by the initial pass) and returned early, leaving nothing behind. Fix: the stale removal is now deferred to just before the actual DOM injection, after all early-return guards have passed. Every early-return path (no comment span, no i[title], empty alias text) now leaves the existing #mb-h1-alias-line untouched. An explicit debug log message is emitted when the function returns early due to the comment span being absent.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: H2/H3 header hover background color is now configurable via sa_ui_h2_h3_hover_bg (default #f9f9f9, matching the original MusicBrainz grey). The value is injected into the .mb-toggle-h2:hover, .mb-toggle-h3:hover CSS rule in the main style block, replacing the previous hard-coded #f9f9f9.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: H1 comment-span relocation split into two independent feature flags: sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_initial_page (default true) controls the run on the original MusicBrainz entity page before any 'Show all' action; sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_final_page (default true) controls a second run after the consolidated table has been rendered. The one-shot IIFE was converted to a named reusable function applyH1CommentSpanRelocation(callSite) so both call sites share identical logic. The final-page call is placed at the end of finalCleanup(), ensuring it fires after every render pipeline (main fetch, Load from Disk, filter re-render). Both settings live in the EXPERIMENTAL FEATURES section of configSchema. The old single setting sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation has been removed.

Configurable H2 section-header background color (sa_ui_h2_bg, default #fff3e0 light orange): background-color is now injected into the .mb-toggle-h2 CSS rule via the settings value. The existing grey hover background (#f9f9f9 on .mb-toggle-h2:hover) is preserved and takes precedence.
Configurable H3 sub-table-header background color (sa_ui_h3_bg, default #f0fff4 light green): background-color injected into .mb-toggle-h3 the same way. Hover background preserved.

Configurable table column-header row colors: three new settings added to the UI APPEARANCE section — sa_ui_thead_th_bg (default #e8e8e8, header row background), sa_ui_thead_th_color (default #333333, header row text color), sa_ui_thead_filter_row_bg (default #f4f4f4, per-column filter row background). The defaults match the original MusicBrainz grey palette visible on the initial entity pages. All three are wired into the applyStickyHeaders() injected style block, replacing the previous hard-coded #f2f2f2 / #f9f9f9 values.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Accidentally dropped click event listener on the sub-table resize button (mb-subtable-resize-btn): during the Change 8 cssText update in 9.99.136 the btn.addEventListener('click', …toggleSubTableAutoResize…) block was omitted from the new_str of the str_replace, causing the resize button to render correctly but silently ignore all clicks. The listener has been restored.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: Global retry button (mb-caa-toggle-btn-global-retry / mb-eaa-toggle-btn-global-retry) height and press effect: the global retry button created inside _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton now receives the same inline style additions as the per-table retry button — display:inline-flex, align-items:center, box-sizing:border-box, transition:transform 0.1s,box-shadow 0.1s. The CSS height-unification selector group was extended with [id$='-global-retry'] (covers both CAA and EAA global retry buttons) and a matching :active press rule was added.

Button press-down effect extended to dialogs: showDownloadNotification Close button receives transition + mousedown/mouseup/mouseleave handlers for the press animation (CSS :active cannot be used reliably on a transient popup). showLoadFilterDialog buttons (Load Data, Cancel, Filter Data, Render Data, history-toggle ▾) receive transition:background-color 0.2s,transform 0.1s,box-shadow 0.1s in their inline style attributes; :active rules for all five were added to the lazily-injected sa-load-popup-styles block.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: Configurable H1 comment-span relocation (sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation, default true): the relocateH1CommentSpan IIFE that extracts the alias <i title='...'> block from the h1's <span class='comment'> and injects a dedicated italic alias line below the page title is now guarded by a new boolean setting in the Experimental Features section. When disabled the h1 DOM is left completely untouched; standard disambiguation comments such as '(live)' that carry no <i title> are never affected regardless of this setting.

Configurable eventParts synthetic column extractor (sa_enable_event_parts_extractor, default true): a new boolean setting in the Experimental Features section controls whether the 'eventParts' extractor entry declared in a page definition's syntheticColumnExtractors array is honoured at runtime. When disabled, buildActiveSyntheticColumnExtractors() silently drops all eventParts entries via a filter step before the extractor list is returned, so none of the synthetic columns Event-Type, Event-Date, Event-Detail, Event-Venue, Event-Venue-Detail, Event-City, Event-State, Event-Country are created. All other extractor types (splitCountryDate, splitLocation, splitArea, …) are unaffected.

Button press-down visual feedback (all h1/h2/h3 action buttons): every script-injected button now produces a tactile translateY(1px) + inset box-shadow press effect on :active. H1 action-bar buttons already carried transition:transform 0.1s,box-shadow 0.1s via uiActionBtnBaseCSS(); the new CSS rule #mb-show-all-controls-container button:active fires them. H2 filter-bar buttons (Toggle highlighting, Clear all COLUMN filters, Clear all filters, global collapse ▶/◀) receive both a transition and an :active rule. H3 sub-table class buttons (mb-subtable-clear-btn, mb-show-all-subtable-btn) likewise. H3 CAA/EAA art toggle buttons (.mb-caa-art-toggle-btn) and their retry siblings ([id^='mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-'], [id^='mb-eaa-toggle-btn-retry-']) and the resize/vis buttons (.mb-subtable-resize-btn, .mb-subtable-vis-btn) all gain :active rules; their inline cssText was extended to include transition:transform 0.1s,box-shadow 0.1s and box-sizing:border-box.

Uniform h3 button height (22 px): all buttons in the h3 sub-table header bar — .mb-caa-art-toggle-btn, .mb-global-art-toggle-btn, [id^='mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-'], [id^='mb-eaa-toggle-btn-retry-'], .mb-subtable-resize-btn, .mb-subtable-vis-btn — are now forced to the same 22 px box-model height (box-sizing:border-box; height:22px; vertical-align:middle). The reference is the CAA toggle button's anatomy: 18 px thumbnail + 1 px top/bottom padding + 1 px top/bottom border = 22 px. The resize and vis buttons additionally received display:inline-flex; align-items:center in their inline cssText so icon/text content is vertically centred within the declared height.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA column-header ▶/◀ button missing on initial render: initCollapsableColumns runs synchronously immediately after renderFinalTable, before any _artEnrichIcon call has resolved. On the initial render the CAA cells are still empty anchors (no ul.mb-caa-art-ul yet), so multiRowCells is empty for the CAA column and no header button is created. On sort re-renders the clones from allRows already carry the built ul structure, so the button appeared. Fix: _artEnrichTable now collects each _caaQueue.enqueue() Promise into an array and returns Promise.all(promises). In _artInitPics, the _artEnrichTable call is chained with .then(() => { if collapsableColumns.includes(ctx.column) → initCollapsableColumns(table) }), so initCollapsableColumns is re-run exactly once after all enrichments for that table have resolved and every CAA cell has its ul.mb-caa-art-ul populated. initCollapsableColumns is idempotent and its art-cell cleanup guards (introduced in 9.99.133) prevent any clobbering of the newly-built cells.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: CAA/EAA column filtering by image type and comment: column filter fields for the CAA/EAA column now match against each image's type strings ('Front', 'Back', 'Booklet', …) and per-image comments. Implementation: new _artBuildSearchText(images) helper concatenates all type strings and comments from an images[] array. _artBuildMultiRowArtCell stores the result in ul.dataset.mbArtSearch (updated on both first-build and rebuild paths). getCleanColumnText() appends this attribute after its normal TreeWalker pass, making the text available to column filters and global RegExp-per-cell filters without modifying getCleanVisibleText() (used for sort keys), which remains unaffected. highlightCrossTag() already walks all text nodes inside the td (it has no FILTER_REJECT), so type badge and comment spans inside collapsed li.mb-caa-art-li-image rows are highlighted when the filter matches — the mb-collapse-toggle-has-match indicator on the toggle cues the user to expand the cell to see the highlighted content.

CAA/EAA column added to collapsableColumns in every applicable page type: all 9 CAA page types (series-releases, label-releases, artist-relationships-filtered, artist-relationships, artist-releasegroups, artist-releases, artist-recordings, releasegroup-releases, recording-releases) and all 3 EAA page types (area-events, place-events, artist-events) now include 'CAA' or 'EAA' in their collapsableColumns array. This gives each of those pages a column-header ▶/◀ button for mass expand/collapse of all art cells, and includes art cells in the global ▶/◀ button. Implementation in initCollapsableColumns: the per-cell loop now skips cells whose <td> contains a direct-child ul.mb-caa-art-ul (they manage their own toggle), but still counts them in multiRowCells so anyColumnHasMultiRow stays true and the header button is created. A new art-cell hidden-match pass after the per-cell forEach stamps mb-collapse-toggle-has-match on art-cell toggles when their collapsed image rows contain filter-highlight spans, matching the same behavior as regular collapsable columns. The header button's click handler naturally finds and clicks the pre-existing art-cell toggles.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sort on any column destroyed the CAA/EAA multi-row art cell collapse state: every sort re-render calls initCollapsableColumns() before initCaaPics(). The idempotent cleanup in initCollapsableColumns() was unconditionally (1) removing all .mb-cell-collapse-toggle spans — deleting the art-cell ▶ toggle, (2) removing mb-has-collapse-toggle from all tds — breaking the CSS positioning context, and (3) resetting all ul > li to display:'' — uncollapsing all image rows. Because initCaaPics() / _artEnrichIcon() run afterwards and skip _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() when multiBuiltAttr is already set on the cloned row (cloneNode(true) preserves dataset), the toggle was never restored. Fix: all three cleanup steps now skip cells whose <td> contains a direct-child ul.mb-caa-art-ul (the marker for art cells built by _artBuildMultiRowArtCell). Art cells retain their cloned toggle, class, and li display state across every sort/filter re-render.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: _artBuildImageLi: added hover-preview wiring to the 18×18 thumbnail inside every per-image <li> in the multi-row CAA/EAA art cell. When sa_caa_hover_preview is enabled, hovering the thumbnail shows the shared singleton popup (same one used by the icon-column anchor and the inline-thumbnail ph span). The preview URL is derived from imgData.thumbnails at sa_caa_big_img_size (fallback chain: configured size → 1200 → large → 500 → imgData.image), so each row shows its own specific artwork — Front, Back, Booklet, etc. — rather than the entity's canonical /front-{size} path. Protocol-relative URL stripping (replace /^http:/) and the crosshair cursor are applied consistently with the other two hover-wiring sites.

sections.label: 🔍 API Research
items: Inspected Cover Art Archive API (coverartarchive.org/release and /release-group endpoints) and Event Art Archive API (eventartarchive.org/event endpoint). Each image in the JSON response exposes: id, types[] (e.g. 'Front', 'Back', 'Booklet', …), front, back, comment, approved, image (original URL), thumbnails.{250,500,1200,small,large}. All new multi-row cell rendering uses thumbnails['250'] with fallback chain 250→small→500→large→image. Protocol-relative URLs (strip leading 'http:') used throughout to avoid mixed-content warnings.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: Multi-row CAA/EAA art cell: the synthetic CAA/EAA column cell now shows a collapsible per-image list built from the full API images[] array. Structure: li-0 (summary row — existing art anchor + count badge + cache hint, always visible) + li-1..N (one per API image, initially collapsed). Each image row shows: 18×18 lazy-loaded thumbnail, type badge (e.g. 'Front / Booklet'), optional comment (italic muted), optional ⏳ pending indicator for unapproved images. Implemented by the new _artBuildImageLi(imgData) helper and _artBuildMultiRowArtCell(ctx, artCell, images) function.

The per-image image rows (li.mb-caa-art-li-image) are wired into the existing ▶/◀ collapse mechanism via the standard .mb-cell-collapse-toggle + ensureCollapseDelegate() infrastructure — no new event listeners or modifications to initCollapsableColumns required.

New _artEnrichTable(ctx, table) function: enqueues _artEnrichIcon for all art anchors in a table, always called from _artInitPics when the CAA/EAA column is present, regardless of the sa_caa_pics_small setting. This separates icon background-image loading from JSON-API enrichment: multi-row cells are populated even when small-icon thumbnails are disabled.

_artInitSmallPics refactored: now only enqueues _artLoadIcon calls (background-image CSS). Enrichment is handled exclusively by _artEnrichTable, eliminating the previous coupling between the small-pics toggle and count badge / multi-row features.

_artEnrichIcon extended: now stores json.images[] in ctx.imagesCache (new per-context Map) alongside the existing countCache entry. On cache-hit re-renders the images array is read from ctx.imagesCache without a network round-trip. After badge/tooltip decoration, calls _artBuildMultiRowArtCell(ctx, artCell, images).

_artRetryTable extended: also purges ctx.imagesCache entries and clears ctx.multiBuiltAttr dataset keys on art cells before calling _artEnrichTable(ctx, table) to force a full API refetch and multi-row rebuild with fresh data.

getCleanColumnText and getCleanVisibleText updated: li.mb-caa-art-li-image elements are now rejected by the TreeWalker filter (FILTER_REJECT), preventing per-image type names and comments from polluting sort keys, filter text, and the unique-values dropdown.

sections.label: 🔍 Analysis
items: Root cause of empty CAA column on artist-relationships pages: MusicBrainz does not render caa-icon / artwork-icon spans in the source HTML of the relationships page — it only shows plain release-title anchor links. The caa() column extractor looks specifically for those artwork-icon spans and finds none, correctly setting the sort-key to 'no' and leaving the CAA column cell empty. The inline thumbnail in the Title column loads via a completely independent path (addCAA: 'Title' → _artInitInlinePics, which queries the CAA API directly from the release GUID embedded in the href attribute — no source-side artwork indicator is needed). Because _artInitSmallPics also needs artwork-icon spans to exist, it has nothing to enqueue for these rows, so no error indicator was ever shown either. Fixing the empty CAA column for these pages would require injecting artwork-icon spans into the synthetic CAA cells when inline thumbnails successfully load — tracked as a separate future enhancement.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Silent failure on artwork icon load error (_artLoadIcon): when a small-icon thumbnail request resulted in an img.onerror (network failure, unreachable host, etc.), the failure was silently swallowed — the CAA/EAA column cell remained blank with no indication to the user. Now a clickable ⚠⟳ error indicator is injected into the .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap/.mb-art-cache-hint-col span position (the same superscript slot used by the Resource Timing cache hints). Clicking the ⚠⟳ triggers a single cache-busted retry of just that one icon (_artLoadIcon with cacheBust=true appends ?_cb=<timestamp> to the URL), removing the error indicator and loading the image fresh from the network. On a successful retry the ⚠⟳ is replaced by the normal Resource Timing cache-hint emoji (🟢/🟡/🔵).

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: Per-sub-table ⟳ retry button (multi-table mode): a ⟳ button is now injected immediately after each per-sub-table CAA/EAA toggle button. Clicking it triggers _artRetryTable(ctx, table, tableIndex) which: (1) adjusts the global badge by subtracting the current sub-table count so the aggregate stays correct as the per-table badge resets and reaccumulates; (2) purges ctx.countCache entries for all entity links in the table so _artEnrichIcon re-fetches image counts from the network; (3) strips enrichedAttr markers so the idempotency guard does not suppress re-fetches; (4) transitions any ⚠⟳ indicators to ⟳ (loading state) for immediate visual feedback; (5) calls _artInitBigPics and _artInitSmallPics with cacheBust=true, appending ?_cb=<timestamp> to every image URL to guarantee a genuine network fetch. _artInitBigPics and _artInitSmallPics now accept an optional cacheBust parameter (default false) for this purpose. Single-table mode also gets the ⟳ button (placed after the toggle button in the h2 header). Implemented by the new _artCreateOrUpdateRetryButton(ctx, table, tableIndex) helper, called from _artInitPics right after _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton.

Global ⟳ retry button on h2 (multi-table mode): mirrors the global toggle button pattern. A single ⟳ button is placed immediately after the global CAA/EAA toggle button in the h2 header. Clicking it calls _artRetryTable for every table.tbl on the page, performing a bulk cache-busted reload of all artwork across all sub-tables simultaneously. Idempotent: created once in _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton and never duplicated.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Global CAA/EAA toggle button count always showed 0: _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton was reading sub-table badge values synchronously at the end of _artInitPics, but those badges all start at 0 and are incremented asynchronously inside img.onload closures in _artInitBigPics. The global badge was therefore always frozen at 0. Fix: the same img.onload block that increments the per-sub-table badge now also looks up the global button (ctx.btnPrefix + '-global') and increments its badge by 1. The global thumbnail is also adopted there if not yet loaded. The global badge is explicitly initialised to '0' on creation (instead of the stale String(totalCount)) and reset to '0' on every re-render pass, so it is always driven entirely by img.onload increments — matching the lifecycle of the per-sub-table badges.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: h3 sub-table collapse (multi-table mode): collapsing a sub-table via the h3 header click now also hides any CAA/EAA art bigbox strips that sit immediately before the table in the DOM. Previously the table rows were hidden but the art strip remained visible, inconsistent with the h2 single-table collapse behaviour. When expanding again, each bigbox is restored to whatever show/hide state the user last set via its individual CAA/EAA toggle button (the tracked caaVisible/eaaVisible dataset attributes are preserved across collapse/expand cycles). The same fix is applied to Ctrl+Click all-h3 collapse and expand. Two new internal helpers implement the pattern: _artHideBigboxesForTable(table) force-hides all bigboxes immediately before a table without touching visAttr; _artRestoreBigboxesForTable(table) restores each bigbox display from its visAttr.

sections.label: ✨ Improvements
items: CAA/EAA toggle button spacing (multi-table mode): increased the CSS gap between the artwork thumbnail icon and the image-count badge in all per-sub-table CAA/EAA toggle buttons from 3 px to 6 px for improved visual separation and readability.

Global CAA/EAA toggle button (multi-table mode): a new 'Globally Toggle' art button is now injected into the h2 header immediately after the total row-count span on multi-table pages (e.g. Artist-Releases). The button mirrors the visual style of the per-sub-table toggle buttons (thumbnail from the first available sub-table image + total image count across all sub-tables). Its tooltip reads 'Globally Toggle CAA/EAA <label> - Show/Hide'. When clicked it simultaneously shows or hides all sub-table art bigboxes for that archive, toggling between show-all and hide-all based on the current aggregate state (any bigbox visible → hide all; all hidden → show all). Individual sub-table toggle button titles are synced accordingly. Implemented by the new _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(ctx) helper, called at the end of _artInitPics for multi-table pages. Idempotent: the button is created once and updated on every filter/re-render pass.

sections.label: 📖 Documentation / Diagnostics
items: Resource Timing cache-hint — 🟡 disk cache is never observed for CAA/EAA archive URLs (documentation and diagnostic enhancement): coverartarchive.org and eventartarchive.org serve their images via a non-cacheable 302 redirect chain to archive.org. The browser must re-resolve this redirect on every page load because the redirect response itself carries Cache-Control: no-store (or equivalent). The PerformanceResourceEntry for the coverartarchive.org URL therefore always records a duration of 2–4 s (the full redirect round-trip plus image transfer), which is permanently above the 5 ms disk-cache threshold. As a consequence, 🟡 (disk cache) will never appear for any CAA/EAA URL regardless of how many times the page is reloaded. The only reliably detectable cached state is 🟢 (memory cache), which appears when the same /front-250 URL for the same release GUID is loaded twice within the same page session (e.g. the big-picture strip and the inline thumbnail both request the same URL). 🔵 (network/redirect) is therefore the expected and correct indicator for every page reload. No classification logic was changed. Changes made: (1) getResourceTimingHint() now also returns redirectStart and responseStart fields (both are 0 for opaque cross-origin responses, confirming the TAO restriction is active). (2) All three debug log lines (icon column, bigbox, inline) now emit the full diagnostic tuple — ts=, ebs=, dur=, redirectStart=, responseStart= — instead of just dur=, making the opaque/redirect nature of the response immediately visible in the developer console. (3) The JSDoc for getResourceTimingHint, the RESOURCE TIMING API settings comment block, and the sa_rt_enable setting description all now document the non-cacheable redirect limitation explicitly.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artEnrichIcon (cache-hint icon column layout): the cache-hint indicator emoji (🟢/🟡/🔵/⚠️) was rendered AFTER the image-count badge instead of ABOVE it. The intended DOM structure is 「anchor → .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap [ hintSpan | countBadge ]」 so the emoji acts as a superscript and the count as a subscript. Root cause: in the badge-creation branch of _artEnrichIcon, anchor.after(badge) inserts the badge immediately after the anchor, pushing it BETWEEN the anchor and the already-existing .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap. The wrapper-adoption step then checked anchor.nextElementSibling which — after anchor.after(badge) — returns badge, not the wrapper; the classList.contains() guard was therefore always false, and wrap.appendChild(badge) never ran. Fix: changed anchor.nextElementSibling to badge.nextElementSibling, which correctly reaches the wrapper sitting just after the freshly-inserted badge.

getResourceTimingHint (Resource Timing cache-hint): every image showed ⚠️ 'Timing unavailable' even on genuine network fetches and even after loading the page a second time (when disk-cache hits were expected to show 🟡). Root cause: coverartarchive.org (and eventartarchive.org) serve their images via a cross-origin redirect chain through archive.org. Per the Fetch/Resource Timing spec, cross-origin redirects without a Timing-Allow-Origin response header cause the browser to zero out BOTH transferSize AND encodedBodySize for the PerformanceResourceEntry — even for a real over-the-wire network fetch. With both sizes = 0 the classification fell through to the final else branch which returned 'unavailable'. For disk-cached images (loaded in < 5 ms, both sizes = 0) the existing heuristic already worked correctly (< 5 ms → 🟡). The problem was only in the final else branch. Fix: changed that branch from status = 'unavailable' to status = 'network'. The 'unavailable' (⚠️) status is now exclusively reserved for the earlier guard where performance.getEntriesByName() returns no entries at all (Performance buffer full, API absent, or image never loaded). After this fix: first page load → 🔵 network; second load from disk cache → 🟡 disk; second load from memory cache → 🟢 memory; no timing entry at all → ⚠️.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Artwork hover preview: hovering over a cover/event art thumbnail now shows a floating full-size preview popup positioned to the right of the thumbnail (or left if near the viewport edge). The feature works in both the CAA/EAA artwork-icon column (hover the artwork anchor element) and in the inline Release/Title column thumbnail (hover the placeholder span). The preview displays the image at the big-picture strip size (sa_caa_big_img_size). Implemented via a singleton <div id='mb-art-hover-preview'> with position:fixed / pointer-events:none appended to document.body; the div is lazily created on first use and reused across all subsequent hovers. Three new internal helpers: _ensureArtHoverPreviewDiv() (lazy singleton init), _positionArtHoverPreview() (viewport-aware placement), _showArtHoverPreview() / _hideArtHoverPreview() (show/hide with setting guard). Controlled by the new setting sa_caa_hover_preview (default: true, in the CAA/EAA section of Settings).

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Resource Timing API cache-hint feature is now fully configurable via a new '⏱️ RESOURCE TIMING API' section in Settings. Four new settings are exposed: sa_rt_enable (master toggle, default true) gates the entire feature; sa_rt_show_icon_column (default true) controls the superscript indicator in the CAA/EAA icon column; sa_rt_show_bigbox (default true) controls the overlay badge on big-picture strip images; sa_rt_show_inline (default true) controls the overlay badge on inline thumbnails. All four default to true (opt-out) so existing behaviour is preserved for users who do not visit Settings.

CAA/EAA icon-column cache-hint repositioned as a superscript above the image-count badge. Previously the hint emoji was prepended inside the artwork anchor itself. It is now placed in a new .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap inline-flex column wrapper (display:flex; flex-direction:column; align-items:center) that sits immediately after the anchor, containing first the .mb-art-cache-hint-col hint span and then the .mb-caa-count-badge / .mb-eaa-count-badge count span. The visual result is a vertical stack — artwork icon | 🟢 | N — where the emoji reads as a superscript and the count as a subscript relative to the icon. CSS rules for .mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap, .mb-art-cache-hint-col, and the contained count badges are injected into the page stylesheet. _artEnrichIcon's anchor.after(badge) call was augmented with a wrapper-adoption step: if the wrapper already exists when the count badge is created (i.e. _artLoadIcon ran first), the badge is immediately moved inside the wrapper via wrap.appendChild(badge); if the wrapper does not yet exist (reverse ordering), _artLoadIcon's adoption code handles it on load.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: CAA / EAA cache-hint indicator (Resource Timing API): all three artwork rendering paths (icon background loads via _artLoadIcon, inline thumbnail loads via _artInitInlinePics, and bigbox strip loads via _artInitBigPics) now probe the browser's Resource Timing API immediately after each image load event fires to classify the response as a memory-cache hit, disk-cache hit, fresh network fetch, or timing-unavailable. Classification follows the standard transferSize/encodedBodySize heuristic (transferSize===0 && encodedBodySize>0 → memory cache; transferSize>0 → network) with a duration<5ms fallback for cross-origin / opaque archive.org responses where both size fields are forced to zero. The result is surfaced as a Unicode emoji badge: 🟢 memory, 🟡 disk, 🔵 network, ⚠️ unavailable. Three new utility functions support the feature: getResourceTimingHint(url) queries PerformanceEntry data and returns a { status, transferSize, encodedBodySize, duration } object; cacheStatusEmoji(status) maps the status string to an emoji; cacheStatusLabel(status, duration) builds a human-readable tooltip string including the round-trip duration in milliseconds. Placement: in the CAA/EAA column icon anchors (_artLoadIcon), the badge is a .mb-art-cache-hint <span> prepended inside the artwork anchor; in the inline thumbnail column (_artInitInlinePics), it is a .mb-art-cache-hint-inline <span> absolutely positioned at the top-left corner of the placeholder; in the bigbox strip (_artInitBigPics), it is a .mb-art-cache-hint-big <span> absolutely positioned at the top-left corner of each wrapper anchor with a semi-transparent background for legibility. All badges carry a title tooltip with the human-readable label. All hint queries and DOM mutations are guarded for idempotency (update existing badge if present, create only once) so re-renders and filter passes do not duplicate the indicator.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: saveTableDataToDisk / highlight spans baked into saved file (multi-table and single-table): after saving data to disk while any filter was active (global filter, sub-table filter, column filter, or pre-filter), the downloaded file contained ephemeral highlight <span> elements inside the serialized cell HTML. On a subsequent 'Load from Disk', those spans were reconstructed verbatim into the new <td> elements by td.innerHTML = cellData.html, causing rows to appear highlighted even though no filter was active after the load. Root cause: saveTableDataToDisk serialized cell data with html: cell.innerHTML which captured the live DOM as-is, including any .mb-subtable-filter-highlight, .mb-global-filter-highlight, .mb-column-filter-highlight, and .prefilter-highlight spans injected by the filter/highlight pipeline. Fix: introduced the helper function getCleanCellHtml(cell) which deep-clones the cell, removes all four highlight span classes from the clone via querySelectorAll + replaceWith(textNode), and returns the cleaned innerHTML. All data-row serialization paths (multi-table group rows and single-table allRows) now call getCleanCellHtml(cell) instead of cell.innerHTML directly. Header cells continue to use raw innerHTML since they never receive highlight spans.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artInitBigPics (CAA / EAA badge count doubles on disk-load, single-table and multi-table): when loading data from disk on an already-rendered page the CAA/EAA toggle-button badge showed 2× the correct count; as soon as the user typed in the filter field it reset to the correct value. Root cause: browser img.onload events are always dispatched asynchronously (even for cached images). When _artInitBigPics is called a second time (e.g. multi-table: once from the disk-load's initial renderGroupedTable(groupedRows) and once from runFilter() → renderGroupedTable(filteredArray); single-table: once from the main fetch pipeline and once from the disk-load's runFilter()), box.innerHTML = '' detaches the first set of elements from the DOM but does not cancel their in-flight load events. Both sets of img.onload handlers fire after JS yields to the event loop; each finds the toggle button via document.getElementById(btnId) and increments the badge — yielding exactly 2× the true count. Fix: introduced a per-button render-generation counter stored in data-art-render-gen on the toggle button element. _artInitBigPics increments the counter (wrapping at 2^31) each time it starts a new render pass and captures the current value (myRenderGen) into a let variable in the enclosing scope. Every img.onload closure checks parseInt(toggleBtn.dataset.artRenderGen) === myRenderGen before incrementing the badge; if a newer render pass has started the closure is a no-op. This invalidates all stale callbacks from prior render passes regardless of how many initCaaPics() calls overlap in the async queue.

updateFilterButtonsVisibility (multi-table, CAA sub-tables): on multi-table relationship pages, toggling the STF (sub-table filter) panel on for a sub-table that has CAA artworks (e.g. 'Liner notes for') and typing a filter string produced no visible effect — the mb-subtable-controls Clear button and '🎨 Toggle highlighting' button did not appear, and the filter-status span was not updated. Sub-tables without artworks (e.g. 'Vocals') were unaffected. Root cause: updateFilterButtonsVisibility located the sibling table via const table = h3.nextElementSibling. When CAA/EAA artworks are present for a sub-table, _artInitBigPics inserts a div.mb-caa-bigbox (or div.mb-eaa-bigbox) immediately before the table element; consequently h3.nextElementSibling returned the bigbox DIV rather than the TABLE, and the guard if (!table || table.tagName !== 'TABLE') return caused the function to exit early before updating any per-sub-table controls. The mb-subtable-controls span existed in the DOM but was never reached. Fix: replaced the bare h3.nextElementSibling assignment with a forward walk that skips non-TABLE siblings (while (table && table.tagName !== 'TABLE') table = table.nextElementSibling) — identical in spirit to findH3ForTable's backward walk past bigbox divs introduced for the h3-lookup direction.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artInitBigPics (CAA / EAA bigbox placement on disk-load, multi-table): after loading data from disk on an already-rendered multi-table page, clicking any CAA/EAA toggle button showed the bigbox below the table instead of immediately below the h3 sub-header, and all bigboxes were stacked consecutively in page order regardless of which button was clicked. Root cause: renderGroupedTable()'s initial cleanup (no-query branch) removes 'h3, table.tbl, .mb-master-toggle' from the container but never touches the bigbox divs (class mb-caa-bigbox / mb-eaa-bigbox). After the old tables and h3s are removed the bigboxes remain as orphaned elements at their previous positions in the container. renderGroupedTable() then creates and inserts fresh h3 + table pairs, followed by initCaaPics() at its tail. _artInitBigPics finds the orphaned bigboxes by their stable ID, clears them (box.innerHTML = ''), and repopulates them — but the existing-box branch never called table.parentNode.insertBefore(box, table), so the boxes were left at their orphaned positions rather than being moved to sit between the new h3 and the new table. Fix: moved the table.parentNode.insertBefore(box, table) call outside the if/else so it runs unconditionally for both new and reused boxes. Node.insertBefore moves an already-in-DOM node when called again, so this is a no-op when the box is already in the correct position and a repositioning operation otherwise.

_artInitBigPics / disk-load pipeline (CAA / EAA badge count doubles on single-table disk-load, triples on multi-table disk-load): when loading data from disk on an already-rendered page, the CAA/EAA toggle button badge showed 2× the correct count on single-table pages and 3× on multi-table pages. Typing any filter query immediately reset it to the correct value. Root cause: the disk-load pipeline contained an explicit unconditional initCaaPics() / initEaaPics() / initCaaInlinePics() / initEaaInlinePics() block (after initBarcodeHighlight()) that duplicated calls already made by other code paths. For multi-table pages, initCaaPics() is called at the tail of renderGroupedTable(); since the disk-load pipeline both awaits renderGroupedTable(groupedRows) and then calls runFilter() (which calls renderGroupedTable(query) internally), initCaaPics() was invoked three times total before the event loop could yield. For single-table pages, runFilter()'s single-table branch calls initCaaPics() directly, and the explicit block called it a second time — two total. Because browser image-load callbacks are always dispatched asynchronously (even for cache hits), all three (or two) sets of img.onload closures were queued before any fired; when the event loop ran they all incremented the same badge, yielding 3× (or 2×) the true count. Fix: removed the explicit initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics block from the disk-load pipeline entirely. Both code paths (runFilter for single-table, renderGroupedTable via runFilter for multi-table) already handle CAA/EAA initialisation correctly and are the authoritative call sites.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (CAA / EAA toggle button layout): after the first filter interaction (global filter, column filter, or sub-table filter), the CAA/EAA toggle button broke its inline layout — the image count number appeared centred below the thumbnail icon instead of sitting to its right. Root cause: the re-render branch (isNew = false, reached on every runFilter() / renderGroupedTable() re-render after the initial load) restored button visibility with `btn.style.display = ''`. Assigning the empty string removes the `display` property from the element's inline style entirely; since the `display:inline-flex` that keeps the thumbnail and count side-by-side was set only via `cssText` on initial creation and lives in the inline style, clearing it caused the button to fall back to the browser default for

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA illustrated discography (single-table mode): cover-art bigboxes, toggle buttons, and inline thumbnails were never initialised on the initial load of any single-table page — including label-releases (which has addCAA: 'Release'), series-releases, and work-recordings-filtered (?direction=…&link_type_id=…). Root cause: the main fetch pipeline contained a comment that said initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics should NOT be called there because renderGroupedTable() already calls them at its own tail. This reasoning is correct for multi-table mode but completely wrong for single-table mode: renderFinalTable() — which is called instead of renderGroupedTable() for all single-table pages — has never called these functions, and no other code path compensated. As a result, every single-table page was silently left without any CAA/EAA initialisation after the initial fetch/render cycle. (The runFilter() single-table branch did call them correctly, so images appeared only after the user typed a filter query or triggered a sort.) Fix: replaced the unconditional skip block with a tableMode-gated call: for tableMode !== 'multi' (i.e. all single-table pages), initCaaPics() / initEaaPics() / initCaaInlinePics() / initEaaInlinePics() are now called in the correct order immediately after initBarcodeHighlight() in the main pipeline. For tableMode === 'multi' they remain absent here (renderGroupedTable() still handles that case) to avoid the doubled-badge-count regression documented in v9.99.118.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: artist-releasegroups (multi-table fetch loop): all release groups were being collapsed into a single “Other” category instead of their real type names (“Album”, “Single”, “EP”, …). Root cause: the fetch loop called `findH3ForTable(table)` on tables from fetched XHR pages. That helper only matches elements that carry the `mb-toggle-h3` class, which is injected by `renderGroupedTable()` on the live page — raw MusicBrainz HTML never has it. `findH3ForTable` therefore always returned null, and every group was labelled “Other”. Fix: replaced the `findH3ForTable` call in the artist-releasegroups fetch loop with a plain backwards walk that matches any <h3> element (no class restriction), exactly as MusicBrainz natively renders release-type headings on fetched pages.

CAA/EAA toggle button: image count badge showed doubled values on the initial multi-table render (e.g. 8 instead of 4 for a 4-row table). Root cause: `initCaaPics()` / `initEaaPics()` / `initCaaInlinePics()` / `initEaaInlinePics()` were called TWICE on every initial multi-table render — once at the tail of `renderGroupedTable()` (where they have always lived) and a second time in the post-render pipeline that follows the `await renderGroupedTable(groupedRows, ...)` call. The second `_artInitBigPics` pass rebuilt each bigbox (`box.innerHTML = ''`), detaching the first set of `` elements from the DOM. Those detached images had already started loading; their `onload` closures captured `btnId` by value and incremented the badge via `document.getElementById(btnId)` even though their wrapper anchors were no longer in the tree. The second set of images also loaded and incremented the badge, yielding exactly 2× the true count. Fix: removed the duplicate `initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics` block from the post-render pipeline. A prominent comment explains why these must NOT be called there.

CAA/EAA toggle button: buttons did not reappear for sub-tables after a global filter was cleared (when the filter had previously reduced those sub-tables to 0 matching rows). Root cause: the stale-bigbox cleanup added in v9.99.117 correctly hides the toggle button via `staleBtn.style.display = 'none'` when a table's link count drops to 0. However, the re-render path in `_artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton` (`isNew = false` branch) only reset the badge counter — it never restored `btn.style.display` to `''`. Any button previously hidden by the count=0 path therefore remained hidden even after rows were restored. Fix: added `btn.style.display = ''` to the re-render branch of `_artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton`, ensuring the button is unconditionally made visible whenever it is updated with a non-zero link count.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: renderGroupedTable (multi-table filter re-render): subtable control buttons (resize ↔️, column-visibility 👁️, collapse ▶, .mb-subtable-controls span) were being injected INTO the CAA/EAA bigbox div instead of the h3 header on filter re-renders. Root cause: the reuse branch resolves the h3 via `table.previousElementSibling`, but after the initial render `_artInitBigPics` inserts a div.mb-caa-bigbox directly before the table, so `table.previousElementSibling` returns the bigbox — not the real h3. All subsequent `h3.querySelector('.mb-subtable-controls')` checks succeeded (bigbox has none), causing every subtable button to be appended to the bigbox.
